explanation: The LTL property states that 'Globally' (G), if the system is in 'PCVMode', then a new breathing cycle must be initiated in the 'next' (X) state if one of two conditions is met. The first condition is that 'breathingCycleDone' (a breathing cycle is over) is true. The second condition is that 'patientBreathingRequest' is true 'and' (&) it is currently in the 'expiratoryPhase'. The '->' (implies) operator ensures that the initiation of a new breathing cycle is a mandatory consequence of these conditions being met while in PCV mode. The 'X' operator precisely captures 'shall be initiated' by indicating that the new cycle starts in the very next time step after the trigger conditions are satisfied.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode, then whenever a new breathing cycle is initiated (indicated by `breathingCycleStart` becoming true), it must have been preceded by one of two specific conditions. The first condition is that a previous breathing cycle was completed (`breathingCycleDone`), and no new breathing cycle had started until that point (`!breathingCycleStart U breathingCycleDone`). The second condition is that a patient requested a new breath during the expiratory phase (`patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase`), and no new breathing cycle had started until that point (`!breathingCycleStart U (patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase)`). The disjunction `|` between these two `U` clauses captures the 'either...or' aspect of the natural language, ensuring that any new breathing cycle initiation adheres strictly to one of these two defined mechanisms. This formulation directly translates 'shall be initiated either after ... or by ...' by specifying the necessary preconditions for `breathingCycleStart`.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), then a new breathing cycle (breathingCycleStart) must be initiated in the very next state (X) if either of two conditions are met: (1) a breathing cycle has just finished (breathingCycleDone), or (2) the patient requests a new breath (patientBreathingRequest) while the system is currently in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase). This captures the requirement that in PCV mode, a new cycle is triggered by either the completion of the previous cycle or by a patient's request during expiration, with 'shall be initiated' implying an immediate transition to the next cycle.

The LTL property states that if the system is in PCV mode, then it must always be the case that if a breathing cycle is over, or if a patient requests a new breathing cycle during the expiratory phase, a new breathing cycle must eventually be initiated. This captures the two conditions under which a new breathing cycle shall be initiated in PCV mode.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode, then a new breathing cycle must be initiated in the very next state if one of two conditions is met: either a previous breathing cycle has just concluded (represented by "breathingCycleDone"), or the patient makes a request for a new breath while still in the expiratory phase (represented by "patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase"). This directly translates the 'shall be initiated either after A or by B' clause into a temporal implication, where the initiation ('breathingCycleStart') is the immediate consequence (X) of the disjunction of the triggering conditions.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, then if a breathing cycle is over (represented by 'breathingCycleDone') OR if a patient requests a new breathing cycle while the expiratory phase is active (represented by 'patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase'), a new breathing cycle must eventually be initiated (represented by 'F breathingCycleStart'). This captures the requirement that in PCV mode, a new breathing cycle is triggered by one of these two specific conditions.

This LTL property captures the requirement that in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) Mode, a new breathing cycle must be initiated immediately after one of two conditions is met. The outer 'G' (Globally) ensures this property holds at all times. The implication 'PCVMode -> (...)' confines the rule to when the system is in PCV mode. Inside the implication, the disjunction 'breathingCycleDone | (patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase)' represents the 'either/or' condition: either a breathing cycle is over, or a patient requests a new breath during the expiratory phase. The 'X' (Next) operator is crucial here as it signifies that 'breathingCycleStart' must be true in the very next state, accurately reflecting the 'shall be initiated' phrasing which implies an immediate consequence rather than an eventual one ('F').

The LTL formula `G (PCVMode -> (breathingCycleStart -> (breathingCycleDone | (patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase))))` captures the requirement. The `G` operator ensures that this property holds globally, i.e., at all times. The outer implication `PCVMode -> (...)` specifies that this rule applies only when the system is in 'Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode'. The inner implication `breathingCycleStart -> (...)` states that if a new breathing cycle is initiated (`breathingCycleStart` is true), then it must be due to one of the specified conditions. The first condition, `breathingCycleDone`, represents "after a breathing cycle is over"; in LTL, without explicit past operators, this is typically interpreted as the completion of the previous cycle being true at the same logical instant a new one begins. The second condition, `(patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase)`, represents "by patient request during expiration", meaning both the `patientBreathingRequest` and `expiratoryPhase` must be simultaneously true. The formula thus constrains the initiation of a new breathing cycle in PCV mode to only occur under these two specified circumstances.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, then any initiation of a new breathing cycle (breathingCycleStart) must be preceded by one of two conditions. The first condition, 'after a breathing cycle is over', is captured by 'X breathingCycleDone', meaning that 'breathingCycleDone' must have been true in the immediately preceding state. The second condition, 'by patient request during expiration', is captured by '(patientBreathingRequest & expiratoryPhase)', meaning that 'patientBreathingRequest' and 'expiratoryPhase' must both be true at the same time the 'breathingCycleStart' occurs. The disjunction ('|') ensures that either of these conditions is sufficient to allow a 'breathingCycleStart'. The implication ('->') ensures that 'breathingCycleStart' only occurs under these specific circumstances, fulfilling the 'shall be initiated either... or...' requirement.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode, then it must be true that if a breathing cycle is over (breathingCycleDone) OR a patient requests a new breath during the expiratory phase (patientBreathingRequest AND expiratoryPhase), then a new breathing cycle will eventually start (F breathingCycleStart). This accurately captures the requirement that a new breathing cycle "shall be initiated" under these specific conditions within the PCV mode.
